endpoints.name: Auth Cookies
endpoints.description: Prepare cookies for auth request

endpoints.name: Auth Request
endpoints.description:
Perform authorization request to get token

Requires cookies from the [POST Auth Cookies] stage. If the user has multi-factor authentication enabled, the response will contain a `type` of `multifactor` and [PUT Multi-Factor Authentication] will need to be used.

Note: 
Authenticating directly is prone to breakage (captchas and cloudflare anti-bot) and does not support users who use alternative sign-in methods.
Consider using [GET Cookie Reauth] or opening a webview to the Riot login page and watching for redirects.

For an example of watching redirects in a webview (using Electron), see [insomnia-plugin-valorant](https://github.com/techchrism/insomnia-plugin-valorant/blob/a8f1db24cacf934d49e7b562b2265c76a8fabdf4/src/util/auth/open-webview-popup.ts)

captcha.description: hcaptcha token from the login page (see <https://docs.hcaptcha.com/>)

endpoints.name: Cookie Reauth
endpoints.description:
Get a new token using the cookies from a previous authorization request  
Use the saved cookies from [PUT Auth Request] (specifically the `ssid` cookie). The auth token and id token can be found from the url this request redirects to.  
  
It's recommended to use this endpoint instead of storing the password and sending it again.  
  
There are ongoing tests at documented at <https://github.com/techchrism/riot-auth-test> that test for auth lifespan using different cookie strategies.  
Currently, it appears refreshing with just the `ssid` cookie is only stable for one week and refreshing with all auth cookies is stable for three weeks.  
  
On a successful response, the 301 redirect location header will be of the format:
> ```https://playvalorant.com/opt_in#access_token={access token}&scope=openid&iss=https%3A%2F%2Fauth.riotgames.com&id_token={id token}&token_type=Bearer&session_state={session state}&expires_in=3600```
  
On an unsuccessful response, the 301 redirect location header will be of the format:
> ```https://authenticate.riotgames.com/login?client_id=play-valorant-web-prod&nonce=1&redirect_uri=https%3A%2F%2Fauth.riotgames.com%2Fauthorize%3Fclient_id%3Dplay-valorant-web-prod%26nonce%3D1%26redirect_uri%3Dhttps%253A%252F%252Fplayvalorant.com%252Fopt_in%26response_type%3Dtoken%2520id_token&response_type=token%20id_token&method=riot_identity```

endpoints.name: Entitlement
endpoints.description: Get entitlement for remote requests with a token

endpoints.name: Multi-Factor Authentication
endpoints.description: Submits a multi-factor authentication code for login

endpoints.name: PAS Token
endpoints.description: Get a PAS token using the auth token. The PAS token is a JWT that contains the affinity for the XMPP server.

endpoints.name: Player Info
endpoints.description: Get the PUUID and other info from a token

endpoints.name: Riot Client Config
endpoints.description: Gets the config file used by the Riot Client. This includes a ton of info, most of it undocumented.

endpoints.name: Riot Geo
endpoints.description: Get the region for a given ID token and auth token. The ID token and auth token can be obtained from [PUT Cookie Reauth]

endpoints.name: Activate Contract
endpoints.description: Activate a specific contract by ID

endpoints.name: Contracts
endpoints.description: Get contract details including agents, battlepass, missions, and recent games

endpoints.name: Item Upgrades
endpoints.description: Get details for item upgrades

endpoints.name: Current Game Loadouts
endpoints.description: Get the current game loadout info for all players in the match

endpoints.name: Current Game Match
endpoints.description: Get the current game match info

endpoints.name: Current Game Player
endpoints.description: Get the current game match ID for the provided player

endpoints.name: Current Game Quit
endpoints.description: Quits the current game

endpoints.name: Account Alias
endpoints.description: Gets the player username and tagline

endpoints.name: Chat Session
endpoints.description: Get the current session including player name and PUUID

endpoints.name: Client Region
endpoints.description: Gets info about the region and locale from the Riot client

endpoints.name: Entitlements Token
endpoints.description:
Gets both the token and entitlement for API usage
`accessToken` is used as the token and `token` is used as the entitlement.

endpoints.name: Friend Requests
endpoints.description: Get a list of friend requests

endpoints.name: Friends
endpoints.description: Get a list of friends

endpoints.name: Local Help
endpoints.description: Get help for the local client

endpoints.name: Local Swagger Docs
endpoints.description: Fetches json Swagger docs for local endpoints. Can be imported into Swagger or Insomnia.

endpoints.name: Local WebSocket
endpoints.description: Connect to the Riot local websocket and subscribe to live JSON API events.

endpoints.name: Presence
endpoints.description:
Get a list of online friends and their activity
  If the player is playing Valorant, `private` is a base64-encoded JSON string that contains useful information such as party and in-progress game score.

endpoints.name: Remove Friend Request
endpoints.description: Removes an outgoing friend request

endpoints.name: RSO User Info
endpoints.description: Get RSO user info

endpoints.name: Send Friend Request
endpoints.description: Sends a friend request to a player. Can be used in conjunction with [GET Friend Requests] and [DELETE Remove Friend Request] to determine a player's PUUID from their game name.

endpoints.name: Sessions
endpoints.description:
Gets info about the running Valorant process including start arguments
  Can be used to get shard, region, and puuid by parsing launch args.

endpoints.name: All Chat Info
endpoints.description: Get information about all active conversations

endpoints.name: Chat History
endpoints.description: Get chat history for all conversations or a specific conversation if the cid is provided

endpoints.name: Chat Participants
endpoints.description: Get information about the participants of all active conversations or a specific conversation if a cid is provided

endpoints.name: Current Game Chat Info
endpoints.description: Get information about the current game chat

endpoints.name: Party Chat Info
endpoints.description: Get information about the party chat

endpoints.name: Pre-Game Chat Info
endpoints.description: Get information about the pre-game chat

endpoints.name: Send Chat
endpoints.description: Send a message to the specified group

cid.description: The conversation ID of the group to send the message to

type.description: Use `chat` for whispers, `groupchat` for group messages, and `system` for system messages

endpoints.description: Change the queue for the party

endpoints.name: Lock Character
endpoints.description:
Lock in an agent  
**DO NOT USE THIS FOR INSTALOCKING**  
Riot doesn't like this. You may get banned or get the API restricted for the rest of us.

variables.name: agent id
variables.description: The agent ID to lock in

endpoints.description: Get Pre-Game loadout data

Select an agent  
**DO NOT USE THIS FOR INSTALOCKING**  
Riot doesn't like this. You may get banned or get the API restricted for the rest of us.

endpoints.name: Account XP
endpoints.description: Get the account level, XP, and XP history for the current player. This endpoint only works with the authenticated player's PUUID.

endpoints.name: Competitive Updates
endpoints.description: Get recent games and how they changed ranking

query.name: startIndex
query.description: The index of the first match to return. Defaults to 0

query.name: endIndex
query.description: The index of the last match to return. Defaults to 10

query.name: queue
query.description: The queue to filter by. Defaults to all queues

endpoints.name: Config
endpoints.description: Get the config for the given player

endpoints.name: Fetch Content
endpoints.description: Get a list of seasons, acts, and events

endpoints.name: Leaderboard
endpoints.description: Get the leaderboard for a given season

query.name: startIndex
query.description: The index of the first entry to return. The client will have this set to 0

query.name: size
query.description: The number of entries to retrieve. The client will request 510 entries

query.name: query
query.description: An optional username to search for

endpoints.name: Match Details
endpoints.description: Get the details of a match after it ends

endpoints.name: Match History
endpoints.description: Get the match history for the given player

query.name: startIndex
query.description: The index of the first match to return. Defaults to 0

query.name: endIndex
query.description: The index of the last match to return. Defaults to 20

query.name: queue
query.description: The queue to filter by. Defaults to all queues

endpoints.name: Name Service
endpoints.description: Get a player's name and tagline by their PUUID. Supports retrieving multiple players in one request.

body_schema.description: PUUID(s) to retrieve the name data for

endpoints.name: Penalties
endpoints.description: Get the matchmaking penalties for the given player

endpoints.name: Player Loadout
endpoints.description: Get the player's current loadout. Only works for your own PUUID.

endpoints.name: Player MMR
endpoints.description: Get a player's MMR and history

endpoints.name: Set Player Loadout
endpoints.description: Set the player's current loadout.

body_schema.description: JSON-encoded player loadout object. See the Player Loadout endpoint for an example. Exclude the Subject and Version properties.

items.description: Guns and knife. Note that the knife (ID: 2f59173c-4bed-b6c3-2191-dea9b58be9c7) does not have charm data (buddies).
